expectations.text: States a formal precondition (e.g., array is sorted, lo <= hi)
expectations.evidence: No formal precondition stated. Only informal note: 'The array must be sorted' but no logical notation or bounds on lo/hi. Missing quantifier syntax.

expectations.text: States a formal postcondition (e.g., returns index i such that arr[i] == target, or -1)
expectations.evidence: No formal postcondition. Informal description: 'Returns -1 if element not found, returns the index if found' but lacks logical structure, no disjunction notation, no specification of what '∃i' means.

expectations.text: Shows derivation steps leading to the code, not just the code
expectations.evidence: Provides finished code directly. Section 'How it works' gives intuition ('dividing array in half') but no derivation from postcondition analysis, no invariant reasoning, no formal steps. Treats code as given, not derived.

expectations.text: Derives test cases from the postcondition (e.g., target present, target absent, boundary)
expectations.evidence: Only 4 basic test cases listed without derivation or postcondition analysis. No mention of base constructors, no connection to postcondition branches, not systematically derived from specification.

expectations.text: The resulting binary search implementation is actually correct (handles all cases)
expectations.evidence: Code is syntactically correct and implements binary search correctly. Handles target found/not found, empty arrays, boundaries. No logic errors in the implementation itself.
